title: B.Sc. (Hons) in Climate Change and Sustainability
content: A Batchelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Climate Change and Sustainability is a degree offered for 3 years that can provide the career for students in various fields which includes higher education, scientific laboratories, industry, government sectors, NGOS and other related discipline. This interdisciplinary degree equips students to understand and respond to the global climate crisis by integrating knowledge from earth sciences, environmental policy, sustainability practices, and green technologies. It promotes systems thinking, ecological awareness, and sustainability leadership, preparing graduates for careers in climate advocacy, environmental management, and international development. 10+2 with Science, with a minimum aggregate of 50% marks. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057) score will be given preference in the merit list. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 To prepare scientific graduates for a lifetime learning in an interdisciplinary and multidisciplinary context, demonstrating quality of excellence, critical thinking, originality, inventiveness, and self-motivation. 02 To prepare graduates with the abilities necessary for diverse professional endeavors who are accepted for employment in various government, business, and research organizations. 03 To prepare graduates with problem solving ability to show excellence in the field of Science that will both advance their personal accomplishments and benefit society as a whole. 04 To prepare graduates capable of moral leadership and teamwork in achieving individual and organizational objectives. 01 Graduates will be able to analyze the scientific, social, and economic dimensions of climate change, including its causes, impacts, and mitigation strategies, to devise sustainable solutions for environmental challenges. 02 Students will gain hands-on experience through fieldwork, internships, and projects, enabling them to implement climate adaptation and mitigation strategies, such as renewable energy solutions, environmental impact assessments, and geospatial analytics. 03 Graduates will be equipped to engage in policy development, environmental advocacy, and stakeholder collaboration, fostering sustainable practices in government, NGOs, or corporate sectors to address global climate and sustainability challenges.
